subject: UX & Research
title: Thumb Zone Design
description: Learn Thumb Zone Design in one minute.

hook.text: Most people use phones with one hand. The thumb can easily reach the lower half of the screen, not the top.

buildup.text: Steven Hoober's research showed that 49% of users hold phones in one hand and navigate with their thumb.

discovery.text: The 'thumb zone' maps easy, stretch, and hard-to-reach areas. Key actions should live in the easy zone.

twist.text: Phones are getting bigger, but hands aren't. The top corners are now unreachable for most users without shifting grip.

climax.text: Bottom navigation bars, floating action buttons, and reachable menus all follow thumb zone research.

punchline.text: Put key actions where the thumb can reach. Bottom beats top.

quiz.question: What does thumb zone research recommend for mobile interface design?
options: Place key actions in the lower half of the screen where thumbs can easily reach
Always put navigation at the top of the screen
Make all buttons the same size regardless of position
